description_long: This bonsai features a robust, thick trunk with visible roots emerging above the soil, giving it a mature and sculptural appearance. The trunk is planted in a round, ribbed ceramic pot with a light gray finish, and the surface of the soil is covered with a layer of natural mulch or straw. The foliage consists of broad, oval-shaped green leaves that are moderately spaced, allowing visibility of the trunk and branches beneath. The branches extend outward in a somewhat radial pattern, with some gentle curves and bends, contributing to an informal upright style. The overall impression is of a healthy, well-maintained bonsai suitable for decorative display in urban environments.
description: A mature bonsai with a thick, sculptural trunk and broad green leaves, planted in a ribbed light gray ceramic pot with visible mulch. The foliage is moderately dense, revealing the trunk's character and informal upright style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad, healthy leaves and the ribbed ceramic pot, with the trunk partially visible beneath the foliage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thick, exposed trunk and roots, as well as the layered mulch covering the soil in the pot.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the trunk's curvature and the arrangement of branches, with the pot's ribbed texture clearly visible.
description_view_3: Angled view displaying the spread of the leaves and the informal, natural branching pattern, with the pot and mulch visible.
attribute_pot_type: Round, ribbed ceramic pot with a light gray color and a smooth, matte finish; no visible decorative patterns beyond the ribbing.
attribute_trunk_curvature: The trunk is thick and gently curved with visible surface roots, following an informal upright style with natural bends and movement.
attribute_branch_arrangement: Branches are moderately spaced and extend outward in a somewhat radial pattern, with gentle curves and a natural, informal arrangement.
attribute_leaf_density: Moderate; the foliage is not overly dense, allowing visibility of the trunk and branches, with some areas more open than others.
